~]S<: Walkthrough for using ForensiX to extract information about a Chrome dump
title: Chrome Forensics with ForensiX
Given a dump of an
%APPDATA%
c
folder from a Windows host, retrieve Chrome usage data.
The above-mentioned folder is taken from the
Digging In The Dump
a
https://github.com/8061xjl/ctfarchive-sctf-3.0/tree/main/files/forensics/digging-in-the-dump
challenge, part of Sieberrsec CTF 3.0.
As my primary operating system is Ubuntu, I skipped tools like
BrowsingHistoryView
a
https://www.nirsoft.net/utils/browsing_history_view.html
and
MiTeC Internet History Browser
a
https://www.mitec.cz/ihb.html
, which are Windows-only.
Searching out on GitHub, I found the bachelors project of
Adam Chmara
a
https://github.com/ChmaraX
ForensiX
a
https://github.com/ChmaraX/forensix
. It is “
Google Chrome forensic tool to process, analyze and visualize browsing artifacts
i
”, built on Docker Compose and with a beautiful, web-based user interface.
Another alternative was
browser-history
c
a
https://github.com/browser-history/browser-history
, a Python3 library and CLI tool which supports the most popular browsers.
Create a folder to dump the multipart archive:
mkdir data
c
Download the challenge files with:
for i in {1..6} do; wget https://github.com/8061xjl/ctfarchive-sctf-3.0/blob/main/files/forensics/digging-in-the-dump/AppData.zip.00$i\?raw\=true -o data/AppData.zip.part$i; done
c
Recreate the original archive from its parts:
cat data/AppData.zip.part* > data/AppData.zip
c
Check the validity of the created archive:
unzip -t ../data/AppData.zip
c
List the files linked to the default Chrome profile:

ls -1 data/AppData/Local/Google/Chrome/User\ Data/Default
'Affiliation Database-journal'
'Affiliation Database'
'Code Cache'
'Download Service'
'Extension Rules'
'Extension Scripts'
'Extension State'
'Feature Engagement Tracker'
'GCM Store'
'Google Profile.ico'
'Local Extension Settings'
'Local Storage'
'Login Data For Account-journal'
'Login Data For Account'
'Login Data-journal'
'Login Data'
'Network Action Predictor-journal'
'Network Action Predictor'
'Safe Browsing Network'
'Search Logos'
'Secure Preferences'
'Session Storage'
'Site Characteristics Database'
'Sync Data'
'Top Sites-journal'
'Top Sites'
'Trusted Vault'
'Visited Links'
'Web Data-journal'
'Web Data'
AutofillStrikeDatabase
blob_storage
BudgetDatabase
Cache
coupon_db
data_reduction_proxy_leveldb
databases
Extensions
Favicons
Favicons-journal
GPUCache
heavy_ad_intervention_opt_out.db
heavy_ad_intervention_opt_out.db-journal
History
History-journal
IndexedDB
LOCK
LOG
Network
optimization_guide_hint_cache_store
optimization_guide_model_and_features_store
Preferences
PreferredApps
QuotaManager
QuotaManager-journal
Sessions
shared_proto_db
Shortcuts
Shortcuts-journal
Storage
VideoDecodeStats
Install ForensiX via
cd forensix && ./install.sh
c
. The process may take several minutes as hundreds of megabytes are downloaded.
Log in with the created account.
By operating ForensiX, the following information can be extracted:
Favicons: Google, Bing, other Singaporean websites
Cached content: stylesheets, scripts, fonts, GIFs.
Chromium Documentation on User Data Directories
a
https://chromium.googlesource.com/chromium/src/+/master/docs/user_data_dir.md
